body:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Export is table-stakes, but this version can still create advantage if we optimize for trust and submission-readiness: chapter-order fidelity, stable formatting presets, and predictable outputs that reduce lock-in anxiety for serious writers.

## Competitor evidence
- Living Writer already markets export options and manuscript portability, so having no reliable export path is a competitive gap we must close: https://livingwriter.com/features
- Dabble positions compile/export as part of the author workflow; users expect clean manuscript outputs with minimal setup: https://help.dabblewriter.com/en/articles/2678070-getting-started-with-compile
- Campfire Pro includes export capability in paid tiers, showing this is now baseline for writing platforms: https://www.campfirewriting.com/pricing

## Risks / trade-offs
- If DOCX/PDF formatting fidelity is inconsistent, this feature damages trust rather than improving it.
- Supporting two formats can increase QA matrix complexity across devices/fonts.
- Scope creep into publishing-suite tooling could delay delivery without clear differentiation return.

## Recommendation
Approve with a quality bar: prioritize deterministic chapter/scene ordering and manuscript-standard formatting correctness over advanced customization. Treat reliability as the differentiator and add explicit validation scenarios for long multi-chapter novels.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
